workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode provides quick, risk-oriented overviews of git diffs for Jenkins console output. It's designed for senior developers who need to quickly understand what changed and where the risks are, without performing a full code review.

step.details: The diff will typically be provided via: - Direct paste in the prompt - File path to a diff file - Git command output

step.action: Parse the diff to identify changed files

tools.tool: read_file - If diff is in a file

step.focus: - File paths and types - Lines added/removed - Context of changes

phase.name: analysis
phase.description: Quickly analyze each changed file for notable changes

steps.step: Identify the type of change (new feature, bug fix, refactor, config change)
Assess the scope and impact of changes

phase.skip_if: - Only whitespace or formatting changes - Only comment changes - Trivial configuration updates

phase.name: risk_assessment
phase.description: Assign risk levels to notable changes

criteria.high_risk: - Changes to authentication/authorization logic - Database schema modifications - Concurrency/threading changes - Security-sensitive code paths - Performance-critical hot paths - Error handling removal or weakening
criteria.medium_risk: - Business logic changes - API contract modifications - New dependencies - Configuration changes affecting behavior - Missing test coverage for new code
criteria.low_risk: - Refactoring with same behavior - Documentation updates - Test additions - Logging improvements

phase.name: output_generation
phase.description: Generate plain text output for Jenkins console

structure.section: Brief overview of what changed (1-3 sentences total)
For each notable change: - File/component name - What changed (1-2 sentences) - Risk level: HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW
Specific concerns to check in detailed review: - Null-safety issues - Concurrency problems - Behavior changes - Security surface expansion - Performance hot paths - Missing tests

completion_criteria.criterion: Output is plain text (no ANSI, no markdown tables)
All notable changes have risk assessments
Output is concise (suitable for console viewing)
If nothing notable, stated clearly in one sentence

case.name: no_notable_changes
case.output: No notable changes detected. Only formatting, comments, or trivial updates.

case.name: large_diff
case.approach: Focus on highest-risk changes first, summarize lower-risk changes in groups

case.name: multiple_files
case.approach: Group related changes, prioritize by risk level
